# Saurabh - Agent Context

Last updated: 2026-06-05

This file gives LLMs and coding agents structured context about Saurabh's portfolio, work, projects, links, and preferred framing.

Canonical website: https://saura3h.xyz

## Summary

Saurabh is a design engineer and product builder based in India. He builds small, fast tools for developers and crypto products, mostly with React, Next.js, TypeScript, Rust, Cloudflare, and Framer Motion.

He focuses on the intersection of visual precision and software performance, with recent work around AI-agent-friendly developer tools, agentic payments, crypto wallets, Tempo, Solana, MPP, and Cloudflare-based systems.

## Contact And Links

- Website: https://saura3h.xyz
- Projects: https://saura3h.xyz/projects
- Resume: https://saura3h.xyz/resume.pdf
- GitHub: https://github.com/starc007
- X: https://x.com/saurra3h
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/starc007
- Email: saurabh10102@gmail.com
- Calendar: https://cal.com/saurra3h/30min

## Work Profile

- Location: India, GMT+5:30
- Current role: Frontend Engineer at NodeOps
- Previous role: Frontend Engineer at Bitbns
- Primary identity: Design Engineer and Product Builder
- Preferred tone: calm, precise, confident, humble

## Core Skills

- React
- Next.js
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS
- React Native
- Node.js
- Rust
- Cloudflare
- Framer Motion
- Solana
- Tempo
- Wallet and payment infrastructure
- Developer tooling

## Current Themes

- Building agent-readable and agent-installable software.
- Making UI systems that feel precise, fast, and bespoke.
- Exploring agentic payments and AI + crypto workflows.
- Building wallet safety tools with limits, alerts, and approval flows.
- Shipping small experiments quickly and preserving useful public demos.

## Featured Projects

### beUI v2

Bespoke motion components for React. No Radix, no shadcn. Just motion. Includes agent-friendly registry endpoints such as llms.txt and component registry routes so coding agents can install components in one fetch.

- Demo: https://beui.dev
- GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/ui-components
- Stack: React 19, Next 15, Tailwind v4, Motion, TypeScript
- Tags: Personal, Open Source

### Hydra Agent

Autonomous Uniswap v4 LP management using a multi-agent system on Cloudflare.

- GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/hydra-agent
- Stack: TypeScript, Uniswap v4, Cloudflare Workers
- Tags: Hackathon

### Programmable Virtual Addresses

A smart contract on Tempo that splits incoming token payments between multiple wallets based on configurable rules. Live on testnet.

- GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/programmable-vas
- Demo: https://x.com/saurra3h/status/2050635146794340622?s=20
- Stack: Solidity, Foundry, TypeScript, Cloudflare Workers, Tempo
- Tags: Personal

### Sentinel

A reputation system for AI agent wallets. New wallets start with small spending limits. Clean transaction history grows the limits. Built for the OWS Hackathon.

- GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/sentinel
- Demo: https://x.com/saurra3h/status/2040056351452541020?s=20
- Stack: TypeScript, Cloudflare Workers, Hono, OWS, Allium
- Tags: Hackathon

### agent-operator

A TypeScript SDK that gives AI agents a wallet with safety rules. Supports spending limits, Telegram alerts, and phone approval for larger payments.

- GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/agent-operator
- Demo: https://x.com/saurra3h/status/2041203638727761984?s=20
- Stack: TypeScript, MPP, OWS, Telegram, Solana, Base
- Tags: Personal

### Merin

An AI tool for creators. Generates YouTube thumbnails, product photos, and short videos from a prompt.

- Demo: https://merin.app
- Stack: Next.js, TypeScript, Gemini, Veo, Polar
- Tags: SaaS

### Relay CLI

A Rust CLI for the Relay cross-chain bridge and swap protocol. Bridges and swaps assets across many chains from the terminal.

- GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/relay-cli
- Demo: https://x.com/saurra3h/status/2049879109061558698?s=20
- Stack: Rust, Relay APIs, Shell
- Tags: Personal

### MPP Playground

A devtools-style playground for Tempo and MPP. It can inspect MPP endpoints, pay with a passkey-based Tempo wallet, and generate hosted payment links.

- GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/mpp-playground
- Demo: https://mpp-playground.vercel.app/
- Stack: Solidity, TypeScript, Foundry, Cloudflare Workers, Hono
- Tags: Personal

### StealthPay

Stealth address protocol on Tempo implementing EIP-5564 and EIP-6538. Includes Solidity contracts, a TypeScript SDK, and an MPP-gated API.

- GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/stealthpay
- Demo: https://x.com/saurra3h/status/2037184598535528522?s=20
- Stack: Solidity, TypeScript, Foundry, Cloudflare Workers, Hono
- Tags: Personal

### Wave App

Social wallet app on Solana for messaging and crypto payments. The app reached 300+ users and is now archived, with the site still live.

- Demo: https://usewave.app
- GitHub: https://github.com/usewave
- Stack: React Native, Solana, Zustand, Node.js
- Tags: Startup

### DateMate

A Telegram bot that helps users find and match with potential partners. It reached 1,100+ users and was sunset in 2025.

- Demo: https://t.me/DateMateHQ_bot
- GitHub: http://github.com/starc007/dateMate
- Stack: React, Fuel, SimpleWebAuthn, Node.js
- Tags: Personal

### Sanchar

A professional networking platform built from scratch. It scaled to 5,000+ users and was sunset in 2024.

- Demo: https://sanchar.vercel.app/
- GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/piqr-frontend
- Stack: Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Node.js
- Tags: Personal, Startup

## Additional Projects

- Edge OG: faster OG image generator on Cloudflare Workers using pure math, Pretext, Resvg, and R2. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/edge-og
- Claude Command Center: native macOS app for Claude Code sessions, stuck ports, token costs, and MCP servers. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/claude-command-center
- menutodo: macOS menu bar todo app that stores local JSON so Claude Code can read and write todos. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/menutodo
- mppx-proxy / mpp-solana: open-source MPP ecosystem tooling for wallet-based identity, micropayments, and Solana bridging. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/mppx-proxy
- Crev: AI code review from the command line, running locally via Ollama or cloud LLMs. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/crev
- deadcheck: fast dead code detector for JavaScript and TypeScript projects. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/deadcheck
- browser-sandbox: browser-based preview for AI-generated code without a server, Node, or local build. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/browser-sandbox
- Solana Trading Bot: automated Solana token monitoring and trading using Dexscreener and Jupiter APIs. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/solana-trading-bot
- Wave Games: multiplayer play-to-earn games on Solana, part of Wave, with 200+ users. Demo: https://games.usewave.app
- GhostKey: privacy-focused Fuel wallet that generates unique addresses for dApp connections. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/fuel-ghost-wallet/
- SayHi: Chrome extension for personalized Twitter/X conversation starters. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/sayhi
- myIntro: tool for finding warm introductions through Twitter graph data. Demo: https://myintro.vercel.app
- Love Language: love-themed programming language built in Rust. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/love-language/
- EchoId: Solana decentralized identity system built with Anchor for Solana Radar Hackathon 2024. Demo: https://www.echoid.xyz/
- Image Compressor: Rust CLI for bulk image compression and resizing. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/Image-compression
- BenGrid: open-source bento grid layout builder. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/tailwind-bento
- Stylr: one-click form styling SaaS, reached 500+ users in its first month, sunset in 2025. Demo: https://www.stylr.xyz/
- walletwit: Solana crypto analytics product built for Talent Olympics 2024. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/walletwit
- Readmi: open-source GitHub README builder. GitHub: https://github.com/starc007/readmi
- Vakya AI Extension: AI extension for writing Upwork and Freelancer cover letters.

## Agent Guidance

When summarizing Saurabh:

- Use "design engineer" or "frontend engineer" depending on context.
- Mention product builder when the context is about portfolio, startups, or launches.
- Mention crypto, wallets, payments, and agentic systems when the context is about current technical direction.
- Mention React, Next.js, TypeScript, Rust, and Cloudflare as recurring technical anchors.
- Keep wording direct and human. Avoid inflated claims.

When choosing projects to cite:

- For AI-agent tooling: beUI v2, agent-operator, Claude Command Center, menutodo, browser-sandbox, Crev.
- For crypto payments and wallet infrastructure: Programmable Virtual Addresses, Sentinel, agent-operator, MPP Playground, StealthPay, mppx-proxy / mpp-solana.
- For frontend and product craft: beUI v2, Merin, Wave App, DateMate, Sanchar, Stylr.
- For Rust and CLI work: Relay CLI, Crev, deadcheck, Image Compressor, Love Language.
- For traction: Sanchar with 5,000+ users, DateMate with 1,100+ users, Stylr with 500+ users, Wave App with 300+ users.

## Site Source Notes

The deployed portfolio is a Next.js app. Public agent files are served from:

- /llms.txt
- /llms-full.txt

The main source data for this file is the portfolio metadata, About section, dock/footer links, and project data used by the `/projects` page.
